title: WF-014 Admin Setting Change Governance — propose -> maker-checker approve+apply -> versioned activation
business_reason: Fourth chain under the workflow-at-a-time rule (WF-014). The scaffold had the change-request + version lifecycle + dualApproval transitions, but proposing a change demanded a client requested_by, and applying a change did NOT actually activate the new version or supersede the old (the governance heart was missing). Make a setting change fully governed: two-person approve AND two-person apply, and apply versions the value.

design.propose: POST /api/staff/admin/settings/changes — creates the next setting_version (proposed value, value_hash, under_review) + a setting_change_request linking it (change_type update, reason, rollback_plan); requested_by SERVER-set (custom route, not a broad generator change). GET lists requests.
design.approve_apply: the generated change-request approve AND apply transitions are BOTH dualApproval (maker-checker — two distinct staff each; self-approval can't apply). Reuses the kernel.
design.activation_effect: server/admin/settingEffects.ts: a domain effect on admin_settings_change_requests:apply (runs after the apply transition applies) ACTIVATES the proposed setting_version (version_status active, effective_from) and SUPERSEDES the previously-active version for that setting_key (effective_to), records a setting_activation_event, audited.
design.ui: custom /staff/admin/settings/changes page (propose form + status list), nav-linked via gen_ui STAFF_CUSTOM_NAV.

param_14.question: How is a setting / feature-flag / policy change governed?
param_14.answer: A staff member proposes a change (new versioned value + a change request, reason + rollback plan; requested_by server-set). It must be APPROVED by two distinct staff and then APPLIED by two distinct staff (maker-checker on both). Applying activates the new version and supersedes the old, records an activation event, and audits — a fully versioned, two-person-governed change.
param_14.answer_source: admin_settings lifecycle (dualApproval approve+apply), setting_versions versioning, WF-014
param_14.security_implications: maker-checker on BOTH approve and apply (two distinct approvers each; self-approval cannot apply); reason on every step; requested_by server-set (never client); changes are versioned (old superseded, never overwritten) + every activation audited.
param_14.approval_implications: a change cannot be applied without two approvals AND two applies; the prior value is retained (superseded) for rollback.
param_14.downstream_dependencies: rollback (activate a superseded version); scheduled activation (effective_from in the future); per-setting value_schema validation of new_value.
param_14.assumptions: an active setting_definition + a current active version exist.
